flags.justification: A purely advisory commission of up to nine members, explicitly required to be bipartisan (Sec. 1(a)).
Specific, sequenced report deadlines (February 15, March 1, and March 15, 1985).
Single purpose (chemical warfare posture review) covering three sequenced, related questions.
A purely advisory body with no compliance regime against third parties.
No compliance regime exists for an incentive structure to attach to.
Sec. 4(b) sets a doubly-bounded termination: thirty days after its report, or September 30, 1985, whichever is earlier.
No existing oversight mechanism is removed.
A purely advisory body with independent, explicitly bipartisan membership.
A narrow, specific-purpose, time-limited commission on a single weapons-policy question.
No second agency's competing mandate is implicated.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.
